name: Dilong
description: The Dilong, also known as the "earth dragon," is a fascinating, lesser-known dragon from Chinese mythology. It represents an early stage in the development of the Chinese dragon, characterized by its terrestrial nature and lack of fully developed dragon features. Unlike the more powerful and majestic dragons that control weather and water, the Dilong dwells on land, often associated with rivers, streams, and underground caverns. It is typically depicted as a serpentine creature, but lacking wings and often horns, features prominent in later dragon depictions. The Dilong's connection to the earth imbues it with powers related to the land, such as controlling the flow of rivers or causing earthquakes. Though less powerful than its celestial counterparts, the Dilong plays a vital role in the balance of the natural world, symbolizing the raw, untamed power of the earth itself. Its existence highlights the evolution of dragon imagery in Chinese culture, from more primal, earthbound creatures to the iconic, sky-dwelling symbols of imperial power.
subtitle: An earth-bound dragon from Chinese mythology.
appearance: Serpentine body, typically without wings or prominent horns. Scaled body, often brown or earthy green. Possesses sharp claws and teeth. Size varies depending on the myth, but generally smaller than celestial dragons.
habitat: Rivers, streams, underground caverns, and mountainous regions.
behavior: Generally reclusive and territorial. Reacts violently to disturbances in its domain. Can be benevolent towards those who respect the earth and its resources.

keyMyths.title: The Dilong and the River's Course
keyMyths.description: A village sought the Dilong's help to redirect a river that was flooding their fields. They offered sacrifices of grain and respect. The Dilong, pleased by their reverence for the land, guided the river to a new, safer course, saving the village from ruin. This story highlights the Dilong's ability to control water flow and its potential benevolence.
keyMyths.source: Local folklore from the Sichuan province

keyMyths.title: The Cave of Echoes
keyMyths.description: A group of treasure hunters ventured into a Dilong's cavern, seeking to plunder its riches. The Dilong, awakened by their greed, caused a series of earthquakes that trapped the treasure hunters within the cave, forever guarding its domain. This myth emphasizes the Dilong's territorial nature and its wrath towards those who disrespect its home.

keyMyths.title: The Farmer's Plea
keyMyths.description: During a severe drought, a desperate farmer prayed to the Dilong for rain. The Dilong, moved by the farmer's sincere plea and the suffering of the land, used its power to draw moisture from the earth, bringing forth a life-saving rain. This tale demonstrates the Dilong's capacity for compassion and its connection to the land's well-being.
